source_jobs.title: Ads/UGC Editor
source_jobs.description:
RESPONSIBILITIES

We’re looking for a creative and performance-driven Video Editor who can help us elevate Triumph’s advertising content across multiple platforms. In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with our marketing and creative teams to produce high-impact video ads that drive engagement, conversions, and excitement for our mobile gaming app.

 - Edit and produce short-form video ads optimized for TikTok, Instagram, and Meta

 - Conceptualize and execute new creative ideas that align with Triumph’s brand and growth goals

 - Highlight key app features, gameplay moments, and winning experiences in an exciting, attention-grabbing way

 - Continuously test and iterate ad variations to maximize performance metrics like CTR, installs, and deposits

 - Collaborate with marketing and data teams to analyze ad performance and implement insights into future edits

 - Maintain a fast-paced workflow while balancing creativity with data-driven decision making

 - Stay on top of creative trends in mobile gaming, user acquisition, and digital storytelling


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2+ years of experience in video editing, ideally with a focus on performance or direct-response advertising

 - Proficiency in editing tools such as Adobe Premiere Pro, After Effects, or Final Cut Pro

 - Strong understanding of social media ad formats and trends (especially TikTok, Instagram Reels, and Meta)

 - Demonstrated ability to create engaging, high-retention ads with strong hooks and clear CTAs

 - Experience working with user acquisition or gaming brands is a strong plus

 - A passion for gaming, competition, and creating content that connects emotionally with players

 - Ability to take feedback constructively and iterate quickly based on performance data

 - Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and comfortable in a fast-moving startup environment


WHY TRIUMPH?

 - Opportunity to build a scaling consumer mobile app that touches gaming, finance, and social (we build all of our games in house).

 - Small, high-impact engineering team that is scaling up. The core engineering team that built the platform is 5 people! Now that we’re scaling rapidly, we’re building a team that can expand the scope of our products and reach. This means you’ll have opportunity to take leadership and grow as an engineer with the company.

 - Passionate team that is proud of our work

 - 16x year over year growth

 - Competitive salary and benefits ($400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.)

All open roles are based out of Triumph HQ in San Francisco, CA but we are opened to remote.

Please send over your editing portfolio to blaise@triumpharcade.com after applying.

source_jobs.title: Operations Associate (Night Shift)
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

As an Entry Level Operations Associate at Triumph (Night Shift), you will play a key role in the operations of our fastest growing app, Rips https://triumpharcade.com/rips, a digital collectible trading card app. You’ll be part of our growing operations team that processes, organizes, and ships Pokémon cards and other collectibles across the country to hundreds of thousands of users. You’ll have the chance to experience first-hand operations at a fast growing startup - from sorting graded cards to uploading inventory into our system. This role is critical to ensuring our users’ happiness and scaling our business as we grow.

We are hiring for a day and a night shift. Please note your schedule preferences in your application as we have some flexibility in regards to scheduling.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Pack and ship cards alongside the broader operations team.

 - Sort, organize, and scan graded Pokémon cards, ensuring inventory is kept accurate and up-to-date.

 - Upload cards into digital inventory using custom-built internal tools.

 - Maintain a secure and well-organized work environment.

 - Contribute ideas and improvements that can help the team scale for the future.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1+ years of experience in an operations, processing, administrative, or other high-performance environment.

 - Availability to work a full time 40-hour weekly schedule.

 - Strong attention to detail, maintaining accuracy and speed even in stressful situations.

 - Scrappy, resourceful, and a strong work ethic - you’re willing to do whatever it takes and take pride in your work.

 - Employment for this position may be contingent upon a background check. With your written authorization, we may obtain reports regarding your employment, education, and, where job‑related, criminal history.
   
   
   


WHY TRIUMPH?

 - High Growth: Contribute to a fast-growing Operations team working to fuel one of the most rapidly growing TCG apps.

 - High Agency: Growing, high-impact Operations team that is scaling rapidly with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High Energy: Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and benefits. healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.

source_jobs.title: Senior Tax Manager / Tax Director
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR TAX MANAGER / TAX DIRECTOR

Location: San Francisco, CA (On-site)

Reports To: VP, Accounting & Corporate Controller

Level: Senior Manager or Director




ABOUT THE COMPANY

Triumph Labs is a rapidly growing consumer gaming and collectibles business operating a peer-to-peer skill-based arcade gaming platform and a rips / collectible card pack draw business. Headquartered in San Francisco, we are scaling domestically and preparing for expansion into Europe, South America, and Australia.




ROLE OVERVIEW

This is a foundational, high-ownership tax hire. You will own the day-to-day of the company's tax function – income, gross receipts, and indirect tax, information reporting, the ASC 740 provision, and the international infrastructure to support our global expansion – and take the lead in managing our external tax firm, directing and pressure-testing their work rather than deferring to it. You will report to the VP, Accounting & Corporate Controller and work in close collaboration with them and the broader finance team, partnering with finance, legal, and operations to embed tax into business decisions from the ground up.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Income, gross receipts & indirect tax. Federal, multi-state, and local compliance and the ASC 740 provision, including apportionment across an expanding nexus footprint, San Francisco Gross Receipts Tax (Prop M classification and NAICS positioning), and sales and use tax, with taxability calls on gaming credits, prize redemptions, and collectible card sales.

 - Estimated tax & planning. Quarterly estimates and prior-year safe-harbor strategy (IRC 6655), NOL preservation including ongoing Section 382 monitoring, and incentives such as the R&D credit (IRC 41) and Section 174 elections.

 - Information reporting & withholding. 1099 versus W-2G determinations for player winnings, constructive-receipt timing on wallet credits and buybacks, and the supporting W-9 and W-8BEN processes.

 - International build-out. Tax structuring for entry into the EU, Brazil, and Australia, including permanent establishment risk, transfer pricing, VAT / GST and gaming duties, and US international provisions (GILTI, FDII, Subpart F, Form 5471).

 - Advisors, provision & audit readiness. Lead the external tax firm as the primary in-house point of contact, build the uncertain-tax-position inventory ahead of the first financial statement audit and eventual public-company reporting, and stand up unclaimed property and escheatment.

 - Process & infrastructure. Govern the sales-tax engine, build scalable compliance processes, and establish the tax calendar, controls, and documentation standards for a function built from scratch.




QUALIFICATIONS

Required.

 - 6-12+ years of progressive corporate tax experience

 - Strong federal corporate income tax and ASC 740 foundation

 - Multi-state income and sales / use tax experience, including apportionment and nexus, and exposure to digital goods, marketplace platforms, or consumer products

 - Track record managing external advisors, with the confidence to direct and challenge a Big 4 team

 - CPA required. MST or LLM in Taxation a plus

Preferred.

 - Gaming, fintech, marketplace, or consumer technology experience

 - Exposure to gross receipts taxes, high-volume information reporting, and VAT / GST regimes (EU, Australia) and international tax structuring concepts (PE risk, transfer pricing basics)

 - Familiarity with skill-based gaming or collectibles and trading-card tax treatment

 - Experience building a tax function at a growth-stage or pre-IPO company




WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE DISTINCTIVE

The tax profile here is genuinely unusual. Skill-based peer-to-peer gaming sits in contested regulatory and tax territory, and the rips and collectibles business adds its own layer of secondary-market and state taxability questions that vary by jurisdiction. A candidate who can navigate ambiguity and engage substantively with novel fact patterns, rather than default

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

As an Engineering Manager at Triumph, you'll lead and grow the team building the systems behind Triumph Arcade and Rips. You'll be accountable for both the people and the product — developing engineers, partnering with product and design on roadmap, and hiring the talent that lets us keep pace with our own growth.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Stay hands-on with the codebase while leading the team - contributing to architecture, pushing code, and managing through the work rather than around it

 - Grow and develop engineers through direct feedback, high standards, and creating clear paths for career progression

 - Own hiring for your team: sourcing strategy, interview process design, and closing candidates who raise the bar

 - Partner with product and design to translate business goals into clear engineering scope, sequencing work to maximize impact and minimize rework

 - Drive technical architecture and system design decisions, balancing long-term scalability with the pragmatism of a fast-moving product environment

 - Lead project execution end-to-end: breaking down ambiguous problems, managing dependencies, and keeping delivery on track without micromanaging

 - Leverage AI tools actively - both in your own work and in setting the standard for how your team uses them to move faster and write better software


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of engineering management experience, with direct people management and hiring accountability

 - Strong technical foundation — you've built backend, mobile, or full-stack systems and can engage substantively with architectural decisions

 - Track record of developing engineers: promotions, lateral growth, performance management when needed

 - Experience in fast-moving consumer product environments where roadmap changes are a feature, not a bug

 - Bonus: experience in gaming, real-money products, or marketplace/trading platforms where trust and reliability are central to the product


WHY TRIUMPH?

 - High growth. Build a high-scale consumer platform that touches gaming, finance, and social with the autonomy to set our web direction.

 - High agency. Small, high-impact engineering team that is growing rapidly with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High energy. Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and benefits. $400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.

Our team gathers 5 days a week at Triumph’s headquarters in San Francisco.

source_jobs.title: Front Desk & Office Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
About the Role:
As we expand our shipping operations to a dedicated warehouse, we're looking for a friendly, organized Front Desk & Office Coordinator to be the first point of contact for visitors, vendors, and staff, while keeping the office running smoothly day to day.

What You'll Do:

 - Greet and check in visitors, vendors, and deliveries at the front desk

 - Answer and route phone calls and general inquiries

 - Order and stock snacks, drinks, and office supplies

 - Manage incoming/outgoing mail and packages

 - Support light administrative tasks (scheduling, filing, data entry)

 - Keep common areas and front office tidy and presentable

 - Coordinate with facilities/property management as needed

 - Assist with ad hoc projects for the operations team


Qualifications:

 - 1+ years of experience in a front desk, receptionist, administrative, or customer-facing role

 - Availability to work a full-time 40-hour weekly schedule on-site

 - Friendly, professional demeanor with strong verbal and written communication skills

 - Highly organized with strong attention to detail — able to juggle multiple requests without letting things slip

 - Comfortable with basic office software (email, calendars, spreadsheets) and willing to learn new tools

 - Scrappy, resourceful, and proactive — you spot what needs doing and take pride in your work

 - Employment for this position may be contingent upon a background check. With your written authorization, we may obtain reports regarding your employment, education, and, where job-related, criminal history.
   

Why Triumph?

 - High Growth: Join one of the most rapidly growing TCG apps as we expand into a dedicated warehouse — and help build the office culture from day one.

 - High Agency: As the face of our office, you'll own the front desk and shape how the workplace runs, with real opportunity to grow into broader operations and administrative responsibilities.

 - High Energy: Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth) — you'll be at the center of it, keeping the office humming.

 - Competitive salary and benefits: healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

As a Product Designer on Rips https://triumpharcade.com/rips, our collectibles trading card app, you’ll collaborate closely with Rips’s Product Manager to shape the future of our mobile and web experience. Your work will build on top of our existing design system and continue to shape one of the fastest growing apps in the App Store. You’ll help deliver a web version of Rips https://apps.apple.com/us/app/rips-by-triumph/id6751921248 and set the foundation for future web and Android apps in our gaming ecosystem. Expect fast cycles of ideating and iterations as you push the boundaries of what it means to be the #1 app in our category.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Ship product design end‑to‑end for Rips: user research, flows, wireframes, prototypes, high‑fidelity UI, and production‑ready assets.

 - Iterate on a mature design system to keep Rips cohesive and evolving.

 - Create interaction patterns that feel native to iOS and scale to web/Android as we expand.

 - Design clear, intuitive surfaces for consumer experiences—especially game‑like mechanics and collectibles.

 - Partner with engineering and product to validate ideas quickly and move from concept to live features in days, not weeks.

 - Contribute to brand moments in‑product (illustration, motion, micro‑copy) that elevate delight and clarity.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1–2+ years of product design experience on consumer-facing iOS apps. Bonus if you’ve worked on gaming, real-money, or other interactive entertainment apps.

 - Expertise designing for iOS/Apple Platform and a desire to work cross-platform as we expand to Android and web.

 - Demonstrated ability to design for consumer engagement (game loops, progression, rewards) and turn complex concepts into delightful user flows.

 - A passion for building delightful user experiences with motion, illustration, animations, and high-quality interaction design.

 - Tenacity, pragmatism, and scrappiness — you value high degrees of agency and know how to prioritize the most impactful work based on data and user feedback.


WHY TRIUMPH?

 - High growth. Build a high-scale consumer platform that touches gaming, finance, and social with the autonomy to set our web direction.

 - High agency. Small, high-impact engineering team that is growing rapidly with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High energy. Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and benefits. $400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.

Our team gathers 5 days a week at Triumph’s headquarters at Levi’s Plaza in San Francisco.

source_jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Role



As Triumph's first dedicated Infrastructure Engineer, you'll own the foundation that everything else runs on. Until now, our infrastructure has been built and maintained by the core engineering team alongside everything else — you'll be the first person whose entire mandate is to make it fast, observable, and bulletproof. Our platform touches gaming, finance, and social at real-money scale, which means there's no margin for downtime. You'll define how we build, ship, and scale from the ground up — CI/CD pipelines, the databases under our highest-traffic paths, and the telemetry that tells us what's actually happening in production. This is a greenfield mandate: you're not inheriting an infra org, you're starting one. As we scale toward our next order of magnitude of growth, you'll set the standards every future infrastructure hire builds on.



What You'll Do

 - Establish Triumph's infrastructure function — set the standards, tooling, and practices that the team and future hires will build on.

 - Own and evolve our CI/CD pipelines, making it fast and safe for a growing team to ship continuously.

 - Take end-to-end ownership of scaling our backend systems, identifying bottlenecks before they become incidents and designing for the next 10x rather than the last one.

 - Drive our database strategy as a deep Postgres expert — query optimization, connection pooling, replication and read routing, schema evolution, and keeping our highest-traffic data paths healthy under load.

 - Build out observability and telemetry so the team can see, understand, and act on system behavior in real time — metrics, tracing, structured logging, and meaningful alerting.

 - Partner with backend engineers to harden the systems that power our core business, from incident response to the architectural decisions that prevent the next one.
   
   

Qualifications

 - Strong experience operating and scaling large production systems, with a track record of true ownership — you don't just build it, you keep it healthy.

 - Deep Postgres knowledge: you understand query planning, indexing, connection management, and what actually breaks at scale (Go and AlloyDB experience is a plus).

 - Hands-on experience designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines.

 - Fluency with observability and telemetry tooling, and an instinct for what to measure.

 - Comfort being the first and only person in a function — you're excited to set direction rather than inherit it, and you drive work to completion without a playbook handed to you.

 - Tenacity, pragmatism, and high agency — you prioritize the most impactful work and drive it to completion.
   
   

Why Triumph?

 - High growth. Build a high-scale consumer platform that touches gaming, finance, and social with the autonomy to set our infrastructure direction.

 - High agency. As our founding infrastructure hire, you'll own a large surface area from day one, with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High energy. Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and benefits. $400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.

Our team gathers 5 days a week at Triumph's headquarters at Levi's Plaza in San Francisco.

source_jobs.title: Staff Accountant
source_jobs.description:
About Triumph

Triumph operates a digital platform for physical collectibles and an arcade-style mobile gaming business. Our flagship app ranks among the top in the gaming category on the App Store, with hundreds of thousands of monthly active users and hundreds of millions of games played. We also operate a collectibles business with real inventory, real fulfillment, and real accounting complexity.



Position Overview

We're looking for a Staff Accountant to join a lean, experienced accounting team and take ownership of core transactional processes from day one. You'll run day-to-day accounts payable and monthly bank and card reconciliations, with review and guidance from the Accounting Manager. It's a strong fit for an early-career accountant who learns fast, works independently, and wants accelerated exposure to the full accounting cycle, including our planned migration from QuickBooks Online to NetSuite.



Key Responsibilities

· Own day-to-day accounts payable: invoice review, GL coding, payment runs, and vendor management in Ramp.

· Perform monthly bank and credit card transaction categorization and reconciliation, subject to Accounting Manager review.

· Review employee travel and expense (T&E) submissions for compliance with company expense policy.

· Prepare routine journal entries, including accruals and prepaid amortization.

· Support the month-end close by preparing schedules, tie-outs, and supporting documentation, and by pulling and organizing data for the team’s more complex reconciliations (payment-processor settlements, wallet and revenue waterfalls).

· Organize workpapers and prepared-by-client (PBC) items for our first external audit.

· Maintain organized, accurate accounting records and files.

· Respond to internal questions about expenses, invoices, and payments.

· Support the planned migration from QuickBooks Online to NetSuite through data entry, validation, and testing.

· Help identify opportunities to improve and streamline accounting processes.



Qualifications

· Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related business field.

· 0-2 years of accounting experience; strong new graduates are encouraged to apply.

· Solid grasp of core accounting principles.

· Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.

· Fast learner who picks up new systems and processes with minimal hand-holding.

· Self-directed: able to run assigned processes with limited day-to-day supervision.

· Strong time management; consistently meets deadlines.

· Works well in a collaborative team environment.

· Proficiency in Excel.

· Experience with QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, or other accounting software a plus.

· Experience with AP or expense-management platforms (e.g., Ramp) a plus.

· Interest in pursuing a CPA license a plus.



Why Triumph?

· High-impact, high-visibility seat on a lean, experienced accounting team, with broad exposure across the full accounting cycle as the function and the company scale.

· A passionate team that is proud of our work.

· Competitive salary and benefits (healthcare, vision, dental, 401(k), and more).

source_jobs.title: Customer Support Agent
source_jobs.description:
As a Customer Support Agent at Triumph, you'll be the front line for the hundreds of thousands of users on Rips, our collectibles trading card app, and Triumph Arcade, our real-money gaming platform. You'll own the user experience when something goes wrong, resolving issues quickly and turning frustrated moments into reasons people stick with us. This is a fully remote role.

What You'll Do

 - Respond to user inquiries across our support channels with speed, empathy, and accuracy.

 - Troubleshoot issues related to games, payments, and in-app features for Rips and Triumph Arcade.

 - Advocate for users internally by surfacing recurring problems so we can fix root causes.

 - Spot trends in user feedback and escalate them so we can stay ahead of issues as we grow.

 - Contribute ideas and improvements that help the support team scale for the future.

Qualifications

 - Digital experience on any platform. You're comfortable navigating apps, tools, and online systems and can learn new ones quickly.

 - Availability to work a full time 40-hour weekly schedule.

 - Strong written communication with empathy and attention to detail, maintaining accuracy and speed even in stressful situations.

 - Demonstrated ability to perform in a fast-paced environment, juggling multiple priorities without sacrificing quality.

 - Previous customer support experience is a plus.

 - Familiarity with the TCG and/or gaming world is a plus.

 - Your own computer and a reliable internet connection.

Why Triumph?

 - Work from anywhere. A fully remote role with a supportive online work environment and a team that has your back.

 - High growth. We promote from within. Our support leadership and managers come from internal employees who started right where you will.

 - High impact. The feedback you gather from users helps shape the product as we scale to hundreds of thousands of users.

 - Competitive pay. $21/hour to start.

source_jobs.title: Media Buyer
source_jobs.description:
RESPONSIBILITIES

We’re looking for an ambitious and data-driven D2C Media Buyer to help us scale Triumph’s user acquisition through paid social and digital advertising. In this role, you’ll manage campaigns across major platforms, run creative tests, and work closely with our marketing and creative teams to drive efficient growth for our mobile gaming app.

 - Plan, launch, and optimize paid media campaigns across Meta, TikTok, Google, and other performance channels

 - Manage budgets and pacing to maximize ROI while maintaining strong unit economics

 - Develop and execute A/B tests on creative, copy, and audience segments to uncover scalable opportunities

 - Collaborate with the creative team to inform ad concepts and provide feedback on performance-driven creative

 - Analyze and report on campaign data to identify trends, insights, and optimization strategies

 - Work closely with product and growth teams to align acquisition goals with user retention and LTV metrics

 - Stay up to date on platform changes, industry trends, and competitor campaigns to maintain a cutting-edge approach


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1–2 years of experience managing paid media campaigns for D2C brands or mobile apps

 - Proven success with Meta Ads Manager and/or TikTok Ads Manager; experience with other channels is a plus

 - Strong analytical mindset with experience using tools like Google Analytics, AppsFlyer, or Looker

 - Understanding of creative testing, conversion optimization, and attribution modeling

 - Comfortable managing budgets and making data-driven decisions in fast-paced environments

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate cross-functionally

 - Passion for gaming, performance marketing, and continuous experimentation

 - Self-starter with a strong sense of ownership and accountability


WHY TRIUMPH?

 - Opportunity to build a scaling consumer mobile app that touches gaming, finance, and social (we build all of our games in house).

 - Small, high-impact engineering team that is scaling up. The core engineering team that built the platform is 5 people! Now that we’re scaling rapidly, we’re building a team that can expand the scope of our products and reach. This means you’ll have opportunity to take leadership and grow as an engineer with the company.

 - Passionate team that is proud of our work

 - 16x year over year growth

 - Competitive salary and benefits ($400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.)

All open roles are based out of Triumph HQ in San Francisco, CA.

source_jobs.title: Creative Designer
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE:

We are seeking a talented and passionate Creative Designer to own the visual identity of the Triumph brand. This is a foundational role on our marketing team, reporting directly to the Head of Growth. You will be the driving force behind creating a consistent, modern, and compelling brand image across all our marketing channels. While you'll start as a solo designer on the marketing team, you'll work closely with our Lead Designer, Travis, to ensure brand cohesion across the entire company.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Brand Stewardship: Take ownership of the Triumph brand guidelines. You will refine, modernize, and ensure consistency across all visual assets, from our website to our social media presence.

 - Ad Creation: Design a high volume of "crazy good" static display ads for various platforms, including Meta, Google, and programmatic networks. You will be responsible for creating visually stunning ads that drive user acquisition.

 - Merchandise and Physical Products: Lead the design for a new line of merchandise and physical products. You'll have the creative freedom to experiment with different product types and bring the Triumph brand to life in the physical world.

 - Marketing Collateral: Create a wide range of marketing materials as needed by the team. This could include social media graphics, email templates, and more.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - A Killer Portfolio: Your experience level is less important than your portfolio. We want to see a body of work that demonstrates exceptional talent, a strong sense of brand identity, and a modern aesthetic. We're particularly interested in seeing brand remakes, high-quality Photoshop art, and merchandise lines.

 - Tool Proficiency: You are highly skilled in the Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator) and Figma. Experience with After Effects is a plus, but not required.

 - A Collaborative Spirit: You'll be working with a passionate team and will receive briefs from the Head of Growth. You should be able to take direction and collaborate effectively to bring ideas to life.

 - A Modern Eye: You have a keen sense of what makes a brand feel current and exciting. You're ready to take our existing brand assets and elevate them to the next level.




HOW TO APPLY:

Please apply through Ashby. We look forward to seeing your portfolio.




WHY TRIUMPH?

 - High growth. Build a high-scale consumer platform that touches gaming, finance, and social with the autonomy to set our web direction.

 - High agency. Small, high-impact engineering team that is growing rapidly with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High energy. Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and benefits. $400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.



Our team gathers 5 days a week at Triumph’s headquarters in San Francisco.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer (Mobile)

source_jobs.title: Quant Strategist
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

As a Quant Strategist, you'll sit at the intersection of mathematics, computer science, and core business decisions. You'll have a direct impact on all facets of the business, applying your quantitative rigor directly to what drives revenue and player experience. You'll build the models and frameworks that power our most critical business decisions.

You'd be joining a small, high-output quant team (4 people today) that operates more like a trading desk than a data org. We build the mathematical systems that power Triumph's core business: pricing engines, payout distributions, matchmaking algorithms, risk models, and player behavior systems.

The team is led by a former quant trader, and current members left careers in quantitative trading to be here. The problems are as deep and interesting as what you'd find at a quant fund - constrained optimization, adversarial dynamics, real-money risk - but with more ownership, faster feedback loops, and direct impact on what gets built. You ship something, and you see it in the numbers the next day.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Develop and optimize core risk-taking and revenue-generating algorithms — the pricing engines, payout structures, and edge calculations that are the mathematical backbone of Triumph's business.

 - Own the quantitative framework for Rips by Triumph: pricing models, pack economics, and rarity calibration.

 - Design and analyze experiments (A/B tests and beyond) with rigorous statistical methodology.

 - Identify high-leverage quantitative problems across the business and drive them from formulation to production impact.

 - Partner closely with engineering, product, and leadership to translate model outputs into real-time production systems and strategic decisions.

 - Design and build machine learning models that directly inform acquisition spend and product decisions.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor's degree in a quantitative subject: math, physics, computer science, data science, or a related discipline.

 - True depth and mastery over any quantitative domain: probability, statistics, applied machine learning, computer science, or math. We want spiky people who are confident that they are the best in their discipline.

 - Proficiency in Python and SQL.

 - Experience working with large amounts of data.




PREFERRED

 - Prior experience as a quantitative trader or quantitative researcher.

 - Experience in competitive math, physics, or CS olympiads, or a graduate degree in a quantitative discipline.

 - Nationally competitive in any activity. Some members from our company include national champions in debate, Clash Royale, and Poker.




WHY TRIUMPH?

 - High growth. Build a high-scale consumer platform that touches gaming, finance, and social with the autonomy to set our web direction.

 - High agency. Small, high-impact engineering team that is growing rapidly with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High energy. Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and benefits. $400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.



Our team gathers 5 days a week at Triumph’s headquarters at Levi’s Plaza in San Francisco.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer (Fullstack)
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

As a Software Engineer, Fullstack on the web team, you’ll drive revenue and company growth by bringing one of the fastest-growing mobile apps to the web. By owning the end‑to‑end web stack and our internal tools and dashboard, you’ll build our web infrastructure and create new opportunities for business growth. You’ll deliver a web version of Rips https://triumpharcade.com/rips, our collectibles trading card app, and set the foundation for future web and Android. By setting the standards for this new platform, you’ll push the boundaries of what it means to be one of the fastest growing app in our category.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Establish our web platform, setting the foundation and standards for how we build future web and Android apps.

 - Utilize existing backend systems and infrastructure to create Rips https://triumpharcade.com/rips for web in 3-6 months.

 - Define our team’s best practices and standards for React (potential to work on React Native as well).

 - Own all internal tools and our internal dashboard, making improvements that help the team be efficient and better use data to drive decision-making.

 - Partner with backend engineers to design APIs and data models, contributing to API designs and app performance.

 - Elevate UX with thoughtful motion, micro-interactions, and responsive design that feels native.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of professional web experience, with deep expertise in modern React, TypeScript, and Javascript. Bonus if you have experience building in a similar domain — real-time features, payments, authentication, or consumer gaming apps.

 - Proven track record for quickly shipping 0→1 consumer web products or major greenfield initiatives.

 - Experience building delightful user experiences with motion, animations, and high-quality interaction design.

 - Strong frontend fundamentals, an eye for design, and strong product sense. You can take a Figma design and build polished, pixel-perfect UI.

 - Tenacity, pragmatism, and scrappiness — you value high degrees of agency and know how to prioritize the most impactful work based on data and user feedback.


WHY TRIUMPH?

 - High growth. Build a high-scale consumer platform that touches gaming, finance, and social with the autonomy to set our web direction.

 - High agency. Small, high-impact engineering team that is growing rapidly with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High energy. Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and benefits. $400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.

Our team gathers 5 days a week at Triumph’s headquarters at Levi’s Plaza in San Francisco.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer (Backend)
source_jobs.description:
THE RIGHT CANDIDATE

The right candidate is passionate about building and has the ability and agency to drive projects to completion.

You should be excited by the prospect of owning a large part of our engineering system, and continuously looking for more responsibility to take on.

Being a backend engineer at Triumph is both demanding and rewarding, and your scope will cover (but not be limited to):

 - Building consumer facing features to help Triumph's core business metrics

 - Solving difficult data drive problems, such as optimal matchmaking, anti-fraud, etc.

 - Iterating on engineering processes to allow the growing team to ship fast - eg. experimentation platforms, improving CI/CD

Above all, we hire people for their slope, who are willing to match the pace and energy of the growth curve of the company. While we ask a lot, we regularly double down on our top people.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of experience as a backend engineer (Go is nice)

 - Strong product sense

 - A strong personal slope and desire to grow one's career.


WHY TRIUMPH?

 - High growth. Build a high-scale consumer platform that touches gaming, finance, and social with the autonomy to set our web direction.

 - High agency. Small, high-impact engineering team that is growing rapidly with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High energy. Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and benefits. $400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.

Our team gathers 5 days a week at Triumph’s headquarters in San Francisco.

source_jobs.title: Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

As a Data Scientist, you'll own the quantitative systems that drive how millions of real-money players experience Triumph's products, from their first session to long-term retention and monetization. You'll build the models and frameworks behind our most critical business decisions: how we price, how we pay out, how we match players, and how we grow.

You'd be joining a small, high-output quant team (4 people today) that operates like a trading desk. We build the mathematical systems that power Triumph's core business: pricing engines, payout distributions, matchmaking algorithms, risk models, and player behavior systems. Every model we ship touches real money and real users. You see the impact in the numbers the next day.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Monetization & Pricing: Develop and optimize the pricing engines, payout structures, and edge calculations that are the mathematical backbone of Triumph's revenue. Own pack economics, rarity calibration, and pricing models for Rips by Triumph.

 - User Journey & Retention: Build models that map the full player lifecycle: acquisition, activation, engagement, monetization, churn risk. Identify the quantitative levers that move retention and LTV, and design interventions that act on them.

 - Experimentation: Design and analyze experiments (A/B tests and beyond) with rigorous statistical methodology. Own the measurement framework that tells us what's actually working across the product.

 - Behavioral Modeling: Develop ML and statistical models on rich, high-frequency user behavior data (session patterns, spend curves, matchmaking outcomes, gameplay trajectories) to drive both product decisions and real-time production systems.

 - Growth & Acquisition: Build models that directly inform acquisition spend and channel optimization, connecting upstream marketing decisions to downstream LTV and monetization outcomes.

 - Cross-Functional Impact: Partner closely with engineering, product, and leadership to translate model outputs into shipped features and strategic decisions. Identify high-leverage quantitative problems across the business and drive them from formulation to production impact.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor's degree in a quantitative subject: math, physics, computer science, statistics, economics, or a related discipline.

 - True depth and mastery in at least one quantitative domain: probability, statistics, applied ML, causal inference, or mathematics. We want spiky people who are confident they are among the best in their discipline.

 - Proficiency in Python and SQL.

 - Experience working with large-scale user or behavioral datasets.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in consumer tech, gaming, fintech, or marketplace data science, particularly in monetization, LTV modeling, or experimentation.

 - Prior experience as a quantitative trader or quantitative researcher.

 - Experience in competitive math, physics, or CS olympiads, or a graduate degree in a quantitative discipline.

 - Nationally competitive in any activity. Some members of our team include national champions in debate, Clash Royale, and Poker.


WHY TRIUMPH?

 - High growth. Build a high-scale consumer platform that touches gaming, finance, and social with the autonomy to set our web direction.

 - High agency. Small, high-impact engineering team that is growing rapidly with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High energy. Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and benefits. $400/mo lunch credit, healthcare, vision, dental, 401k, etc.

Our team gathers 5 days a week at Triumph’s headquarters at Levi’s Plaza in San Francisco.

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

Triumph is a skill-based real-money gaming platform in a period of rapid growth. Real money flows through every game, every matchmaking decision, and every payout, which means the data infrastructure powering those systems has to be fast, reliable, and built to scale.

As our first dedicated data engineering hire, you'll own the full data stack: ingestion, transformation, warehouse architecture, pipeline reliability, and the systems that connect model outputs back to production. You'll work at the intersection of a quantitative strategy team and a fast-moving engineering org, building the foundation that both depend on.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Architect and own the data warehouse. Design and optimize our BigQuery environment for performance, cost, and reliability as data volumes scale with user growth.

 - Build and maintain transformation layers. Own our dbt project end-to-end, including models, testing, documentation, and CI/CD, turning raw event streams into clean, trusted datasets.

 - Pipeline orchestration. Build and manage robust data pipelines with proper orchestration, monitoring, alerting, and failure recovery. Nothing should break silently.

 - Real-time data systems. Design and implement streaming infrastructure for use cases where batch processing falls short: live game economics, real-time risk signals, and in session player behavior.

 - Reverse ETL and production integration. Close the loop between model outputs and the product by getting scores, segments, and predictions back into production systems where they drive real decisions.

 - Data quality and reliability. Build the testing, validation, and monitoring frameworks that let a small team trust the data at scale.

 - Partner with DS and engineering. You'll sit between two teams that move fast and need different things from the data layer. Translate between them and make both more productive.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals. You write clean, maintainable, well-tested code.

 - Deep experience with SQL and Python in production data contexts.

 - Hands-on experience with data warehousing (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, or similar) and transformation frameworks (dbt strongly preferred).

 - Experience building and operating data pipelines with orchestration tooling (Airflow, Dagster, Prefect, or similar).

 - Understanding of data modeling patterns (dimensional modeling, slowly changing dimensions, incremental materialization).

 - Ability to work independently and make sound architectural decisions. You'll have a lot of autonomy and you need to use it well.




PREFERRED

 - Experience with streaming/real-time data systems (Kafka, Pub/Sub, Flink, or similar).

 - Familiarity with analytics engineering and the modern data stack (Fivetran, Statsig, or similar tools).

 - Exposure to quantitative or financial data environments where correctness and latency matter.

 - Experience being an early or first data engineering hire. You've built from zero before and know what to prioritize.




WHY THIS ROLE

You'd be building and owning the entire data engineering function at a hypergrowth consumer startup where data runs through every layer of the business. Every product decision, every dollar of revenue, and every player interaction flows through the stack you'll build. You'll set the architecture, choose the tooling, define the standards, and see your work become load-bearing infrastructure from day one. If you want to build something from scratch at a company that lives and breathes data, this is a rare opportunity




WHY TRIUMPH?

 - High growth. Build a high-scale consumer platform that touches gaming, finance, and social with the autonomy to set our web direction.

 - High agency. Small, high-impact engineering team that is growing rapidly with significant opportunity for leadership and growth.

 - High energy. Passionate team who are proud of our work and velocity (16x year over year growth).

 - Competitive salary and

notes: Triumph Arcade is a gaming and collectibles company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles. The company has a strong engineering core, but also has a significant number of non-tech roles in operations, marketing, and finance.
